findings.lessonTitle: Lesson 15.1: Connecting Frontend to Your API
Lesson 15.3: REST API Design - Professional Standards
Lesson 15.4: Error Handling from Database to UI
Lesson 15.5: Deployment from Laptop to Production

issues.description: The lesson describes the deployment pipeline but doesn't mention environment-specific configuration management (dev/staging/prod profiles) in detail
issues.webResearchNote: While Spring profiles are mentioned elsewhere, a dedicated section on managing environment-specific configs (application-dev.yml, application-prod.yml) would strengthen this lesson.

findings.lessonTitle: Lesson 15.6: Complete Feature - Database to UI
Lesson 15.7: Virtual Threads and Project Loom

issues.originalText: Spring Boot 4.0 enables virtual threads by default
issues.description: The content states virtual threads are enabled by default in Spring Boot 4.0. This is correct for Spring Boot 4.0.x when running on Java 21+, but should clarify the requirements
issues.webResearchNote: Spring Boot 4.0.x enables virtual threads by default ONLY when running on Java 21 or later. For Java 25, this is true. Should add note about Java version requirement.

issues.originalText: Every @RestController handler runs on a virtual thread
issues.description: While true, should mention that virtual threads can be disabled via configuration and how to verify they're active
issues.webResearchNote: Students can verify virtual threads are active by logging Thread.currentThread() in a controller - virtual threads show 'VirtualThread' in the name.

issues.description: The benchmark numbers (5x improvement) are reasonable but should note that actual results vary significantly based on workload type, database latency, and hardware
issues.webResearchNote: Add disclaimer: 'Results from controlled benchmark. Your results may vary based on workload characteristics, database latency, and infrastructure.'
